---
title: "Modular Shiny Applications"
subtitle: "Design, Development, Reactivity, and Scale"
author: "Brian Yandell ([_byandell.github.io_](https://byandell.github.io))"
date: last-modified
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    transition: slide
    slide-number: true
    show-slide-number: all
    embed-resources: true
    footer: "Modular Shiny Applications"
    highlight-style: github
---

# Reactive Complexity & Design {background-color="#e0f2fe"}

## The Challenge of Large Shiny Apps {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
### Why Modularize?
- Shiny apps are highly interactive, but large apps can become complicated and frustrating to debug.
- Single apps longer than **500 lines** often suffer from subtle paradoxes caused by reactive elements.
- Modularization breaks code into self-contained components (like functions) to allow piece-by-piece testing and reuse.
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
::: {.callout-note}
### Core References
- **[Mastering Shiny](https://mastering-shiny.org)**: Chapters 19 on [Shiny Modules](https://mastering-shiny.org/scaling-modules.html).
- **[Modularizing Shiny app code](https://shiny.rstudio.com/articles/modules.html)** (RStudio).
:::
:::
:::

- For multi-language comparisons, see python vs R implementations in the [Shiny Geyser App](https://github.com/byandell/geyser).

## Key Repositories Overview {.compact-slide}

Several packages and applications illustrate an evolving design pattern aimed at improving usability, performance, and debuggability:

- **[helperApps](https://github.com/byandell/helperApps)**: General-purpose modules designed for direct reuse in other Shiny apps.
- **[qtlApp](https://github.com/AttieLab-Systems-Genetics/qtlApp)**: Hierarchical module design for QTL visualization at scale.
- **[geyser](https://github.com/byandell/geyser)**: Learning sandbox showcasing simple module connection patterns.
- **[foundrShiny](https://github.com/AttieLab-Systems-Genetics/foundrShiny)**: Multi-portal package driven by collaborator layout requirements.
- **[qtl2shiny](https://github.com/byandell-sysgen/qtl2shiny)**: Multi-module app for local genomic analysis.
- **[shiny_module](https://github.com/byandell/shiny_module)**: Interactive repository for testing early reactivity concepts.

# Case Studies: qtlApp & geyser {background-color="#e0f2fe"}

## qtlApp: QTL Visualization at Scale {.compact-slide}

Designed for QTL analysis at scale, organized as an R package with separate shiny modules.

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
### Calling Hierarchy
- **`qtlServer.R`**: Top-level server logic.
  - **`mainParServer.R`**: Shared parameters.
    - **`traitServer.R`**: Dataset display and returns `trait_list`.
  - **`scanServer.R`**: Reads scans from files.
  - **`peakServer.R`**: Reads peaks from files.
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
### Infrastructure & Setup
- **`qtlSetup.R`**: Standard initialization script to locate and load files into the environment.
- **`app.R`**: Deployable entry-point script calling the modules.
- **Helper Scripts**: Decouple visualization (`QTL_plot_visualizer.R`) and analysis (`trait_scan.R`, `peak_finder.R`).
:::
:::

## geyser: Modular Concepts {.compact-slide}

Uses the standard Old Faithful geyser app to illustrate module composition and connection logic:

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Module Library (geyser/R)
- **Plots**:
  - `hist.R` / `gghist.R`: Base & ggplot histogram.
  - `ggpoint.R`: Scatterplot modules.
- **Data**:
  - `data.R` / `datasets.R`: Data loader modules.
- **Connections**:
  - `rows.R` / `wrapper.R` / `switch.R`: Connect controllers.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Connection Layout Apps
- **Over Pages**: `appPages.R`.
- **Rows & Columns**: `appRows.R` / `appRowsModule.R`.
- **Twin Reuse**: `appTwin.R` / `appDupe.R` / `appFlip.R`.
- **Nested**: `app.R` (see deployed [Geyser Demo](https://connect.doit.wisc.edu/geyserDemo) ⚠️).
:::
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
See the [Geyser Shiny Modules Slides](https://connect.doit.wisc.edu/geyserShinyModules) and [11 Dec 2024 Presentation](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1BGSIhihpBc-2TfRza5RGeXBCB55EC6-l) for walk-throughs.
:::

# foundrShiny: Collaborator-Driven Architecture {background-color="#e0f2fe"}

## foundrShiny: Lab Portals {.compact-slide}

Powers three active research portals for the Attie Lab:

::: {.incremental}
- **Study Portals**: [Founder Calcium Study](https://connect.doit.wisc.edu/FounderCalciumStudy/) ⚠️, [Founder Diet Study](https://connect.doit.wisc.edu/FounderDietStudy/) ⚠️, and [Founder Liver Diet Study](https://connect.doit.wisc.edu/FounderLiverDietStudy/) ⚠️.
- **Dependency**: Leverages the [foundr](https://github.com/AttieLab-Systems-Genetics/foundr) data analysis package.
- **Scale**: Over **30 interconnected modules** (e.g. `download.R` for exports, `volcano.R` for plots).
- **Iteration**: Evolved over **1.5 years** through collaboration on layouts, panel structures, and feature additions.
:::

## Parameter Scoping & Hierarchy {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
### Input Scoping Levels
To prevent code duplication, inputs are scoped at three distinct levels:
1. **`mainPar`**: Shared parameters (e.g. dataset choice, height) globally available.
2. **`panelPar`**: Localized settings for a specific dashboard panel (e.g. strains, sex, facet overlays).
3. **`plotPar`**: Specific options limited to visualization plots.
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
### calling hierarchy
- **foundr**: mainPar, about, download, entry, contrast, stats, time, trait
  - **contrast**: panelPar, contrastGroup, contrastTime, contrastTable, contrastTrait, timePlot
    - **contrastGroup**: contrastPlot
    - **contrastTrait**: contrastPlot
  - **stats**: panelPar, contrastPlot
  - **time**: panelPar, timePlot, timeTable
  - **trait**: panelPar, corPlot, corTable, traitNames, traitOrder, traitPairs, traitSolos, traitTable
:::
:::

::: {.callout-note}
### Reactive Data Flow
Inputs are passed directly into the
[foundr](https://github.com/AttieLab-Systems-Genetics/foundr)
module. The application avoids global variables; instead, static inputs are filtered into small reactive subsets based on user panels.
:::

## Decoupling Reactivity from Analysis {.compact-slide}

A key architectural shift was completely separating data analysis from the reactive Shiny interface.

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Legacy Layout
- **Mixed Code**: Early versions (such as the [foundr v1.4 branch](https://github.com/byandell-sysgen/foundr/tree/v1.4)) mixed reactivity, analysis calculations, and plotting configurations in single files.
- **Risk**: Hard to debug, poor test coverage, and fragile scaling.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Decoupled Layout
- **Analysis Package ([foundr](https://github.com/AttieLab-Systems-Genetics/foundr))**: Houses clean, non-reactive R code (ggplot structures, mathematical steps).
- **Shiny Package ([foundrShiny](https://github.com/AttieLab-Systems-Genetics/foundrShiny))**: Manages UI wrappers, reactivity, session states, and client connections.
:::
:::

- **R Helpers**: Analysis helpers are strictly organized by category (e.g. plotting, summary, calculation, and utilities).

# qtl2shiny: Scaling & Modernization {background-color="#e0f2fe"}

## qtl2shiny: Local QTL Analytics {.compact-slide}

Designed for local QTL investigations within a 1-4Mb peak region, including LOD scans, SNP association, and mediation:

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
### Server Refactoring Tree
The modernization branch (`byandell-refactor`) reorganizes server calls:
- **`mainServer.R`**
  - **`dashServer.R`**
    - **`setupServer.R`**: project, pheno, peaks.
    - **`haploServer.R`**: probs, snpSetup, scanCoef, mediate.
    - **`diploServer.R`**: pairProbs, snpSetup, pattern.
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
### Modernization Goals
- **Dashboard Upgrades**: Transition older screens from `shinydashboard` layouts to modern, responsive `bslib` dashboards.
- **Consolidation**: Eliminate duplicate download routines using the unified `download.R` module.
:::
:::

- See screenshots and vignettes in the [User Guide](https://github.com/byandell/qtl2shiny/blob/master/vignettes/UserGuide.Rmd) ⚠️ and [Developer Guide](https://github.com/byandell/qtl2shiny/blob/master/vignettes/DeveloperGuide.Rmd).

## Dependencies & Reactive Debugging {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### qtl2 Package Stack
- [qtl2](https://kbroman.org/qtl2/): Genotyping and mapping calculations.
- [qtl2ggplot2](https://github.com/byandell-sysgen/qtl2ggplot2) ⚠️: ggplot2-based plotting extension.
- [qtl2fst](https://github.com/byandell-sysgen/qtl2fst): Database storing scans.
- [qtl2pattern](https://github.com/byandell-sysgen/qtl2pattern): SNP association patterns.
- [qtl2mediate](https://github.com/byandell-sysgen/qtl2mediate) /
[intermediate](https://github.com/byandell-sysgen/intermediate): Mediation calculations.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Sandbox Learning
- **[`shiny_module`](https://github.com/byandell/shiny_module)`package`**:
  - Sandbox designed to understand core reactivity rules and event sequences.
  - Useful reference for tracing reactive logs and resolving unresponsive inputs.
:::
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
### Version Notes
The original
[qtl2shiny](https://github.com/byandell-sysgen/qtl2shiny)
repository contains legacy module syntax. Active retooling is recommended to migrate functions into compliant server/UI wrappers for easier testing.
:::
